name: 11-factor-cli
title: 11-factor-cli
summary: Audit and refactor command-line products so they are installable, understandable, scriptable, observable, testable, and release-ready.
recommended_trigger_phrases: Use the 11-factor-cli skill to audit this CLI for installability, command UX, configuration, error handling, logging, testing, packaging, and release readiness.
expected_inputs: package metadata such as package.json, pyproject.toml, Cargo.toml, go.mod, or setup config
Example commands, tests, workflows, and release scripts when available
expected_outputs: CLI audit report with evidence and score
Refactor plan ranked by user impact and release readiness
Command UX improvements with example commands
Open issues, assumptions, and next iteration suggestions
review_protocol: Identify the CLI runtime, entrypoint, installation path, and core commands.
Run or statically inspect help output, examples, config handling, and error messages.
Score the CLI against the 11-factor checklist.
Prioritize fixes that make the CLI installable, testable, predictable, and scriptable.
Return an audit report and concrete refactor plan.
